response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with broad full-stack experience and notable GIS/geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map, which aligns with the navigation/geospatial domain. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary language, and the resume shows only limited C/C++ exposure from a 2014-2015 stint at National Instruments, with no Rust. Core experience is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than the production C++/Rust systems work this routing tiling role demands.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Senior-level engineer with low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (Sociomantic)
Linux proficiency and DevOps/distributed systems familiarity
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (only ~6 months in 2014-2015) and no Rust
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not systems-level languages
No demonstrated navigation/routing pipeline or map tiling experience
